title: Unify skip transport with the stable seek path
summary: 0.4.30 publishes `Fa.Player.11.var` with a narrow transport fix: skip-forward and skip-backward now use the same seek-and-resume helper as scrub, and the diagnostics probe now logs skip request and completion details to the normal VaM log.
reasoning: Live testing on `0.4.28` narrowed the failure seam further. Repeated slider scrubs appeared stable, but a skip-ahead button press could still leave playback stuck and contaminate later media loads. That behavior strongly suggested the transport buttons were still using a narrower runtime path than scrub. This slice keeps scope tight and simply routes skip through the already-hardened seek helper so skip inherits the same pause, motion-reset, audio-apply, and resume behavior as scrub.

changes.summary: Changes standalone skip-forward and skip-backward to call the same stable seek helper that slider scrub already uses.
changes.reasoning: The old skip path wrote `videoPlayer.time` directly and refreshed only the paused frame, which left it more fragile than the scrub path on problematic media.

changes.summary: Adds `skip_request`, `skip_ok`, and `skip_failed` probe logging at the transport boundary.
changes.reasoning: If a media-specific edge case still survives after the path unification, the next witness run should say exactly what the skip button attempted and whether playback resumed cleanly.

changes.summary: Publishes the transport-fix candidate under a fresh semantic version and `Fa.Player.11.var` package identity.
changes.reasoning: A new rung keeps the witness clean and avoids colliding with `main`, which already uses `0.4.29` for unrelated work.

verification: Stamp version 0.4.30 into player.version.json and BuildRuntimeInfo.cs, then build `Fa.Player.11.var` from the release worktree.
Verify the package contains the 0.4.30 assetbundle, plugin, preset, and demo scene.
Load `Fa.Player.11.var`, play a long video, scrub repeatedly, then use skip-forward and skip-backward to confirm transport jumps no longer leave playback stuck.
If the issue survives, enable the diagnostics probe and capture the normal VaM log so the skip events can be compared directly against scrub events.
knownIssues: The diagnostics probe remains hidden and scene-configurable rather than user-facing.
This slice does not yet add a dedicated diagnostics scene variant; it only strengthens the transport path and the existing probe output.
